businessSummary.oneLiner: World's largest EV maker and 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomy, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08, though Q4 operating margin contracted to 5.7%. FSD's $99/month subscription model is ramping recurring revenue, while April 2026 brings Cybercab production launch and Roadster unveiling as key growth catalysts.
howTheyMakeMoney: Energy storage and generation (Megapack, Powerwall, Solar)
Services and charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Vs S&P 500 avg ~23x, auto sector avg ~12x. Motley Fool warns 'this multiple demands near-perfect execution, yet profits are shrinking'

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS estimate of $2.08. Q4 2025 operating margin fell to 5.7% from 6.2% YoY

keyMetrics.name: Q1 2026 Delivery Consensus
keyMetrics.interpretation: April 2 release, D-1. Seeking Alpha sees 370K+ beat possible but warns of margin erosion from price cuts

keyMetrics.name: EPS (FY2025)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Motley Fool: 'Q4 EPS plunged 60% YoY. Profitability is heading in the wrong direction'

keyMetrics.name: Europe Sales Rebound
keyMetrics.value: First YoY increase in a year
keyMetrics.interpretation: ACEA data shows first YoY registration increase since Dec 2024. Factory retooling base effect is significant

keyMetrics.name: RSI (14-day)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Near oversold threshold of 30. Four consecutive down days. Pre-market bouncing today on Trump war-ending signal

growthDrivers.title: Trump signals willingness to end Iran war (3/31, today)
growthDrivers.description: WSJ reports Trump indicated willingness to wind down hostilities. S&P futures +1.1%. Potential oil decline is a double-edged sword for Tesla: less EV switching incentive but macro stabilization benefits valuations

growthDrivers.title: Europe sales grew YoY for first time since Dec 2024 (3/31)
growthDrivers.description: Motley Fool reports ACEA data showing first YoY registration increase in a year. Largely a base effect from early 2025 retooling shutdowns, but signals European market recovery

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab April production + Roadster late-April unveiling
growthDrivers.description: Steerless fully autonomous robotaxi production launch and next-gen Roadster unveiling both scheduled for April. Two major catalysts concentrated in one month

growthDrivers.title: EV market share 50%+ despite 28% industry decline (3/27)
growthDrivers.description: Tesla expanded market share above 50% even as overall EV market declined 28%. Brand power and Supercharger network prove more valuable during downturns

growthDrivers.title: Refresh Semi spotted at Giga Texas for first time (3/31)
growthDrivers.description: Refreshed Tesla Semi spotted at Giga Texas on Q1's final day. Signals commercial truck production preparation and product line expansion

growthDrivers.title: Oil at $100 could accelerate EV transition
growthDrivers.description: WTI above $100 with US gas approaching $4/gallon. CarGurus identifies $4 as the psychological tipping point where EV search interest surges. Tesla's 50%+ share makes it the primary beneficiary

competitiveAdvantage.summary: US EV market 50%+ share, 8.4B FSD miles data, Supercharger NACS standard dominance, Terafab AI chip in-housing

moats.description: 8.4 billion real-world FSD driving miles. Impossible for competitors to replicate at this scale in the near term
Supercharger network adopted as NACS standard. Ford, GM, and others now use Tesla's charging infrastructure
Battery cells, AI chips (Terafab), software, and charging infrastructure all in-house. Cost advantage and rapid innovation
Synonymous with EVs. High brand recognition and passionate fan base generate demand without marketing spend

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: China market leader, 100%+ growth in Europe. Price competitiveness is catching up fast

competitors.name: Zoox (Amazon)
competitors.detail: Expanding robotaxi in SF, Vegas, Austin, Miami. Direct robotaxi competitor

competitors.name: Waymo (Alphabet)
competitors.detail: Ahead on autonomous commercialization with 200K+ weekly paid rides

competitors.name: Hyundai/Kia
competitors.detail: Global EV share expansion with Ioniq and EV6. Competing on value and quality

recentNews.headline: Trump signals willingness to end Iran war (WSJ). S&P futures surge +1.1%
recentNews.significance: WSJ reports Trump willing to wind down military operations even with Strait of Hormuz largely shut. Market-wide rally. Potential oil decline is a double-edged sword for EV demand but macro stabilization is the bigger positive

recentNews.headline: Motley Fool: 'How Much Further Could Tesla Stock Fall?' P/E 330x, EPS -47% analysis
recentNews.significance: Stock down 20%+ in 2026. P/E 330x requires perfect execution but profits are declining. Q4 operating margin 5.7%, EPS -60% YoY. Fundamental valuation concern persists

recentNews.headline: Tesla grew Europe sales for the first time in a year (Motley Fool)
recentNews.significance: ACEA data shows first YoY registration increase since Dec 2024. Significant retooling base effect but signals European market turning point

recentNews.headline: Seeking Alpha: Tesla may beat Q1 deliveries but margin erosion is the real risk
recentNews.significance: 370K+ delivery beat possible but price cuts and incentives could hurt margins. 'Selling more but earning less' scenario

recentNews.headline: Refresh Tesla Semi spotted at Giga Texas for the first time
recentNews.significance: Refreshed Semi sighting at Giga Texas on Q1's final day signals commercial truck production preparation

analystOpinions.notableComment: HSBC $119 (low) vs Wedbush $600 (high). Seeking Alpha: delivery beat possible but margin erosion is the real risk. Motley Fool: 'How much further can it fall?'

risks.title: Q1 deliveries tomorrow (4/2), margin erosion peak concern
risks.description: Seeking Alpha: 370K+ possible but price cuts may erode margins. Motley Fool: Q4 operating margin 5.7% (down from 6.2%), FY2025 EPS -47%. Delivery beat with margin miss is the worst-case combo

risks.title: P/E 329x with declining earnings, valuation unjustifiable
risks.description: Motley Fool titled 'How Much Further Could Tesla Stock Fall?' FY2025 EPS $1.08 (-47%), deliveries -9% YoY. P/E 330x assumes perfect execution while profits are contracting

risks.title: War ending could lower oil prices, reducing EV switching incentive
risks.description: If Iran war ends, oil could drop from $100+, reducing the urgency for EV adoption. Short-term negative for Tesla demand, though macro stabilization helps valuations

risks.title: FSD regulatory and safety risks intensifying
risks.description: NHTSA investigation upgraded. Camera-only system under scrutiny. Trefis warns the '$1T AI thesis could collapse'

risks.title: Below both 50-day and 200-day SMAs, RSI 31 oversold
risks.description: Below 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). RSI 31 oversold. Pre-market bouncing but trend reversal needs confirmation

buyReasons.title: Trump war-ending signal + RSI 31 oversold = bounce catalyst
buyReasons.rationale: Biggest macro risk (Iran war) potentially resolving. RSI 31 oversold with S&P futures +1.1%. Technical bounce imminent after four consecutive down days

buyReasons.title: Europe sales rebound + Q1 delivery 370K+ surprise possible
buyReasons.rationale: First European YoY increase in a year combined with Seeking Alpha's 370K+ delivery forecast could improve sentiment. Tomorrow (4/2) is the reveal

buyReasons.title: April catalyst concentration (4/2 deliveries, Cybercab, Roadster, 4/21 earnings)
buyReasons.rationale: Multiple major catalysts packed into April could trigger short-term momentum reversal

buyReasons.title: Refresh Semi at Giga Texas, product pipeline expansion
buyReasons.rationale: Commercial truck market entry diversifies revenue. Giga Texas sighting signals production preparation

overallOpinion: Trump signaled willingness to end the Iran war (WSJ), sending S&P futures up 1.1%. For oversold Tesla at RSI 31, this could be the bounce catalyst. Potential oil decline is a double-edged sword for EV demand, but macro stabilization is the bigger positive

Tomorrow's Q1 delivery report (4/2) is the key event. Seeking Alpha sees a 370K+ surprise but warns margin erosion tags along. Motley Fool asks 'how much further can it fall?' noting P/E 330x with profits declining. Europe sales grew YoY for the first time since Dec 2024, a modest bright spot

At $355.28, below both 50-day ($387) and 200-day ($400). Today's macro bounce plus tomorrow's potential delivery surprise could trigger short-term reversal. But if margins disappoint, upside is limited. Hold, reassess after 4/2 deliveries

investmentVerdict.summary: Trump's war-ending signal sparks a macro bounce, but tomorrow's Q1 deliveries could go either way on margins. RSI 31 oversold with Europe sales rebounding is encouraging. However, P/E 330x with EPS -47% remains the fundamental problem. Wait for 4/2 delivery data and 4/21 earnings

sources.name: Bloomberg - Trump signals Iran war ending, S&P futures +1.1%
sources.description: WSJ: Trump willing to wind down hostilities. S&P +1.12%

sources.name: 247WallSt - S&P surges on Trump war-ending news
